title: myriophyllum indicum subsp. tetrandrum
intro: Myriophyllum indicum subsp. tetrandrum, also known as the Indian water milfoil, is a subspecies of aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This aquatic plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Indian water milfoil include its potential to treat various health conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Indian water milfoil can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
